# Basic Usage

# Plays back an mcp file
mcp-replay sample.mcp
stdout -count=2 -q 'jsonrpc'

## Alternate syntax
mcp-replay -f sample.mcp
stdout -count=2 -q 'jsonrpc'

## Writing to a file
mcp-replay -f sample.mcp -o output.mcp
cmp -q output.mcp sample.mcp

## Raw JSON mode
mcp-replay -f sample.mcp -json
! stdout 'mcp-recv'
stdout -count=2 '^{"jsonrpc"'

# Verbose mode and speed flag
mcp-replay -v -speed=2.2 sample.mcp
stderr -count=1 -q 'replaying sample.mcp at 2.2x speed'

cmp -q output.mcp sample.mcp
## Writing to an invalid file fails
! mcp-replay -f sample.mcp -o .

## Running bare fails with usage
! mcp-replay

## Opening non-existent files fails
! mcp-replay -f non-existent.mcp

-- sample.mcp --
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","id":1} # 1683000000.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"server":true}}} # 1683000000.550
